codeReview.summary: The feature covers the requested auth/header/base-URL shaping, but it resolves environment-backed config lazily on every request instead of freezing it into the client, so a supposedly immutable `OpenAI` handle can silently change credentials, routing headers, and base URL after construction.

issues.description: `ClientConfig::resolve()` re-reads `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_BASE_URL`, `OPENAI_ORG_ID`, and `OPENAI_PROJECT_ID` from process environment every time it runs, and `OpenAI::prepare_request()` / `resolve_request_options()` call it on each request. That means an `OpenAI::new()` or env-backed client does not actually capture deterministic config at construction: later `std::env` changes can flip auth, org/project routing, or base URL underneath an existing client, which violates the feature contract for deterministic env loading and the architecture rule that the root client owns immutable shared state.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `sdk-core-worker` still assumes a brainstorming approval flow that does not map cleanly onto mission exec mode, so workers keep recording a procedural deviation even when they invoke the required skill stack.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/sdk-core-worker/SKILL.md:16-18,24-25` makes `brainstorming` and strict TDD mandatory for shared-core client changes; the handoff at `/Users/staticpayload/.factory/missions/beaa247f-5975-4da4-acc2-19deef388841/handoffs/2026-04-05T14-40-02-816Z__client-config-auth-and-headers__b82b99ba-9535-4340-9963-a148b3e703ff.json` records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure = false` and explicitly asks to clarify the brainstorming approval gate for non-interactive exec mode.

summary: Fail: the feature implements the visible config/header behavior, but env-backed clients are not actually immutable because configuration is reloaded from process environment on every request. I also noted the existing sdk-core-worker skill-doc mismatch around brainstorming/TDD procedure in non-interactive exec mode.
